services_snapshot.name: ADPSvc
services_snapshot.description: This service is used for Aggregated Data Platform scenarios.

services_snapshot.name: ALG
services_snapshot.description: Provides support for 3rd party protocol plug-ins for Internet Connection Sharing

services_snapshot.name: AppIDSvc
services_snapshot.description: Determines and verifies the identity of an application. Disabling this service will prevent AppLocker from being enforced.

services_snapshot.name: Appinfo
services_snapshot.description: Facilitates the running of interactive applications with additional administrative privileges.  If this service is stopped, users will be unable to launch applications with the additional administrative privileges they may require to perform desired user tasks.

services_snapshot.name: Apple Mobile Device Service
services_snapshot.description: Provides the interface to Apple mobile devices.

services_snapshot.name: AppReadiness
services_snapshot.description: Gets apps ready for use the first time a user signs in to this PC and when adding new apps.

services_snapshot.name: AppXSvc
services_snapshot.description: Provides infrastructure support for deploying Store applications. This service is started on demand and if disabled Store applications will not be deployed to the system, and may not function properly.

services_snapshot.name: ApxSvc
services_snapshot.display_name: Windows Virtual Audio Device Proxy Service
services_snapshot.description: Manages virtual audio devices for Windows-based programs.  If this service is stopped, virtual audio devices will not function properly.  If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start

services_snapshot.name: AudioEndpointBuilder
services_snapshot.description: Manages audio devices for the Windows Audio service.  If this service is stopped, audio devices and effects will not function properly.  If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start

services_snapshot.name: Audiosrv
services_snapshot.description: Manages audio for Windows-based programs.  If this service is stopped, audio devices and effects will not function properly.  If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start

services_snapshot.name: autotimesvc
services_snapshot.description: This service sets time based on NITZ messages from a Mobile Network

services_snapshot.name: AxInstSV
services_snapshot.description: Provides User Account Control validation for the installation of ActiveX controls from the Internet and enables management of ActiveX control installation based on Group Policy settings. This service is started on demand and if disabled the installation of ActiveX controls will behave according to default browser settings.

services_snapshot.name: BDESVC
services_snapshot.description: BDESVC hosts the BitLocker Drive Encryption service. BitLocker Drive Encryption provides secure startup for the operating system, as well as full volume encryption for OS, fixed or removable volumes. This service allows BitLocker to prompt users for various actions related to their volumes when mounted, and unlocks volumes automatically without user interaction. Additionally, it stores recovery information to Active Directory, if available, and, if necessary, ensures the most recent recovery certificates are used.  Stopping or disabling the service would prevent users from leveraging this functionality.

services_snapshot.name: BFE
services_snapshot.description: The Base Filtering Engine (BFE) is a service that manages firewall and Internet Protocol security (IPsec) policies and implements user mode filtering. Stopping or disabling the BFE service will significantly reduce the security of the system. It will also result in unpredictable behavior in IPsec management and firewall applications.

services_snapshot.name: BITS
services_snapshot.description: Transfers files in the background using idle network bandwidth. If the service is disabled, then any applications that depend on BITS, such as Windows Update or MSN Explorer, will be unable to automatically download programs and other information.

services_snapshot.name: Bonjour Service
services_snapshot.description: Enables hardware devices and software services to automatically configure themselves on the network and advertise their presence.

services_snapshot.name: brave
services_snapshot.description: Keeps your BraveSoftware software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your BraveSoftware software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no BraveSoftware software using it.

services_snapshot.name: BraveElevationService
services_snapshot.description: Provides elevated privileges for Brave.

services_snapshot.name: bravem
services_snapshot.description: Keeps your BraveSoftware software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your BraveSoftware software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no BraveSoftware software using it.

services_snapshot.name: BrokerInfrastructure
services_snapshot.description: Windows infrastructure service that controls which background tasks can run on the system.

services_snapshot.name: BTAGService
services_snapshot.description: Service supporting the audio gateway role of the Bluetooth Handsfree Profile.

services_snapshot.name: BthAvctpSvc
services_snapshot.description: This is Audio Video Control Transport Protocol service

services_snapshot.name: bthserv
services_snapshot.description: The Bluetooth service supports discovery and association of remote Bluetooth devices.  Stopping or disabling this service may cause already installed Bluetooth devices to fail to operate properly and prevent new devices from being discovered or associated.

services_snapshot.name: camsvc
services_snapshot.description: Provides facilities for managing UWP apps access to app capabilities as well as checking an app's access to specific app capabilities

services_snapshot.name: CDPSvc
services_snapshot.description: This service is used for Connected Devices Platform scenarios

services_snapshot.name: CertPropSvc
services_snapshot.description: Copies user certificates and root certificates from smart cards into the current user's certificate store, detects when a smart card is inserted into a smart card reader, and, if needed, installs the smart card Plug and Play minidriver.

services_snapshot.name: ClickToRunSvc
services_snapshot.description: ‪Manages resource coordination, background streaming, and system integration of Microsoft Office products and their related updates. This service is required to run during the use of any Microsoft Office program, during initial streaming installation and all subsequent updates.‬

services_snapshot.name: ClipSVC
services_snapshot.description: Provides infrastructure support for the Microsoft Store. This service is started on demand and if disabled applications bought using the Microsoft Store will not behave correctly.

services_snapshot.name: COMSysApp
services_snapshot.description: Manages the configuration and tracking of Component Object Model (COM)+-based components. If the service is stopped, most COM+-based components will not function properly. If this service is disabled, any services that explicitly depend on it will fail to start.

services_snapshot.name: CoreMessagingRegistrar
services_snapshot.description: Manages communication between system components.
